title: New Technology Changes Lives for People with Diabetes
subtitle: An artificial pancreas is helping patients manage their health more easily and fairly.

summary.en: A new medical device called an artificial pancreas is now available through the NHS in England and Wales. It uses advanced technology to automatically control insulin levels for people with type 1 diabetes. Early results show that this system is reaching a diverse group of patients, reducing previous gaps in healthcare access. Patients report that the device significantly improves their daily lives and reduces the need for hospital visits. Health organizations are continuing to expand the program to reach all eligible individuals.
summary.zh: 一種稱為「人工胰臟」的新型醫療器材現已透過英格蘭與威爾斯的國家醫療保健服務提供。它利用先進技術為第一型糖尿病患者自動控制胰島素濃度。初步結果顯示，該系統正觸及多元的患者群體，縮小了過去在醫療照護取得上的落差。患者表示，該裝置顯著改善了他們的日常生活並減少了住院需求。醫療機構正持續擴大該計畫，以涵蓋所有符合資格的個人。

paragraphs.en: For many people living with type 1 diabetes, managing blood sugar levels is a constant, difficult task. However, a new medical device known as an artificial pancreas is changing how patients handle this condition. According to The Guardian Health, this technology is being rolled out across the NHS in England and Wales, and it is already making a major difference in the lives of thousands of people.
paragraphs.zh: 對於許多第一型糖尿病患者而言，控制血糖濃度是一項持續且艱鉅的任務。然而，一種稱為「人工胰臟」的新型醫療器材正在改變患者應對此病症的方式。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，這項技術正在英格蘭與威爾斯的國家醫療保健服務（NHS）體系中推廣，且已為數千人的生活帶來重大改善。

paragraphs.en: An artificial pancreas, officially called a hybrid closed-loop system, is made of three main parts that work together. First, a sensor worn on the body, called a continuous glucose monitor, tracks sugar levels. Second, a smart computer program—or algorithm—calculates exactly how much insulin the body needs. Finally, an insulin pump delivers that medicine directly into the bloodstream. By handling these tasks automatically, the device removes much of the mental stress that patients usually face during the day and at night.
paragraphs.zh: 人工胰臟正式名稱為「混合式閉迴路系統」，由三個共同運作的主要部分組成。首先，配戴於身上的感測器（稱為連續血糖監測儀）會追蹤血糖濃度。其次，一套智慧電腦程式（即演算法）會精確計算身體所需的胰島素劑量。最後，胰島素幫浦會將藥物直接注入血液中。透過自動化處理這些任務，該裝置減輕了患者平時與夜間通常面臨的許多心理壓力。

paragraphs.en: In the past, new diabetes technology often failed to reach everyone equally. The Guardian Health reports that people from certain ethnic backgrounds or those living in poorer areas often had less access to these tools. However, the first two years of this new program have shown a different result. There is now only a very small difference in how often people from different backgrounds receive the device. This suggests that the current rollout is succeeding in providing fair access to care for all patients.
paragraphs.zh: 過去，新的糖尿病技術往往無法平等地普及於所有人。據《衛報健康》報導，來自特定族裔背景或居住在較貧困地區的民眾，往往較難取得這些工具。然而，這項新計畫推行兩年後的結果卻截然不同。目前，不同背景的民眾在取得該裝置的頻率上，差異已非常微小。這顯示目前的推廣計畫在確保所有患者都能公平獲得照護方面取得了成功。

paragraphs.en: For many users, the impact of this technology is life-changing. Naiha Shafiq, a 27-year-old from London, shared her experience with the device. Before she started using the pump, she frequently ended up in the hospital due to serious complications from her condition. She struggled with the daily routine of giving herself insulin injections, which was especially difficult because of her personal and religious life. Since she began using the artificial pancreas, she has found it much easier to manage her health, and her need for hospital care has dropped significantly.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多使用者來說，這項技術的影響足以改變人生。來自倫敦、二十七歲的奈哈·沙菲克（Naiha Shafiq）分享了她使用該裝置的經驗。在使用這款幫浦之前，她常因病情引發的嚴重併發症而住院。她過去難以應付每日自行注射胰島素的例行公事，這對她的個人生活與宗教信仰而言尤其困難。自從開始使用人工胰臟後，她發現管理健康變得容易許多，住院需求也大幅降低。

paragraphs.en: In 2023, the NHS announced plans to offer this device to more than 150,000 adults and children with type 1 diabetes. So far, the program has focused on children, with about 32,000 young patients already fitted with the system. This represents more than 70 percent of those who are currently eligible. The program is expected to continue over the next few years until every eligible person has the chance to receive the technology.
paragraphs.zh: 二〇二三年，國家醫療保健服務宣布計畫為超過十五萬名第一型糖尿病成人與兒童提供此裝置。目前為止，該計畫重點在於兒童，已有約三萬兩千名年輕患者裝設了此系統。這佔了目前符合資格人數的七成以上。預計該計畫將在未來幾年持續進行，直到每位符合資格的人都有機會獲得這項技術。

paragraphs.en: Experts in the field are encouraged by these early successes. Helen Kirrane, the head of policy and campaigns at Diabetes UK, noted that the device has made a significant difference for many thousands of people. She stated that the organization is proud of the work done to ensure the rollout is fair and focused on the needs of patients. However, she also mentioned that there is still more work to be done to ensure that everyone who needs this technology can get it.
paragraphs.zh: 該領域的專家對這些初步成功感到振奮。英國糖尿病協會（Diabetes UK）政策與運動負責人海倫·基蘭（Helen Kirrane）指出，該裝置已為數千人帶來顯著改變。她表示，該組織對於確保推廣過程公平且聚焦於患者需求的努力感到自豪。不過，她也提到，仍需持續努力以確保所有需要此技術的人都能取得。

paragraphs.en: While the NHS focuses on this rollout, the broader health technology industry continues to look for new ways to use sensors and data to improve patient outcomes. As reported by STAT News, leaders in the medical technology field are exploring how these monitoring systems might be used for other health purposes in the future. This suggests that the technology behind the artificial pancreas could soon lead to even more innovations in how we track and treat various health conditions.
paragraphs.zh: 在國家醫療保健服務專注於此推廣計畫的同時，更廣泛的醫療科技產業也持續尋求利用感測器與數據來改善患者預後的新方法。據《STAT新聞》（STAT News）報導，醫療科技領域的領導者正在探索這些監測系統未來如何應用於其他健康目的。這顯示人工胰臟背後的技術，未來可能引領更多創新，改變我們追蹤與治療各種健康狀況的方式。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the success of the artificial pancreas shows how combining smart technology with a fair distribution plan can improve public health. By reducing the daily burden on patients and ensuring that treatment is available to everyone, the NHS is setting a new standard for diabetes care. As the program expands to include more adults, the positive effects on the lives of patients are expected to grow even further.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，人工胰臟的成功顯示了將智慧科技與公平分配計畫相結合，如何能提升公共衛生水準。透過減輕患者的日常負擔並確保人人皆能獲得治療，國家醫療保健服務正在為糖尿病照護樹立新標準。隨著計畫擴大至更多成人族群，預期對患者生活的正面影響將會進一步擴大。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To officially introduce a new product or service to the public.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「滾動」，這裡指新政策或產品的推行。文中：this technology is being rolled out across the NHS in England and Wales, and it is already making a major difference in the lives of thousands of people.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have a piece of equipment or a device attached to or installed on someone or something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於機械維修，這裡指將醫療器材安裝在患者身上。文中：So far, the program has focused on children, with about 32,000 young patients already fitted with the system.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A heavy load or something difficult that one has to deal with.
keyTerms.noteZh: 指心理或生活上的壓力，而非物理上的重量。文中：By reducing the daily burden on patients and ensuring that treatment is available to everyone, the NHS is setting a new standard for diabetes care.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: NHS rollout of artificial pancreas narrows inequality in diabetes care

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: STAT+: Dexcom CEO hints at future uses for CGM

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the current status of the NHS artificial pancreas rollout regarding children?

options.A: It has reached 150,000 children so far.
options.B: It has been provided to over 70 percent of eligible young patients.
options.C: It is currently only available to children living in wealthy areas.
options.D: It has failed to meet the needs of children due to technical complications.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第五段，NHS 已經為約 32,000 名年輕患者安裝了該系統，這代表了目前符合資格人數的 70% 以上。選項 A 錯誤，因為 150,000 是 NHS 計畫提供的總目標人數（包含成人與兒童），而非目前已達成的兒童人數。選項 C 與文章第三段提到的公平分配目標相抵觸。選項 D 與內文描述的成功經驗不符。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the impact of the artificial pancreas on patients' daily lives?

options.A: It completely eliminates the need for any medical follow-ups.
options.B: It allows patients to ignore their diet and exercise habits entirely.
options.C: It reduces the psychological burden of constant self-monitoring.
options.D: It has replaced the need for insulin for all type 1 diabetes patients.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段，該裝置能自動處理血糖監測與胰島素注射，從而減輕了患者每天面臨的心理壓力（mental stress）。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章並未提到免除醫療追蹤。選項 B 是錯誤的推論，醫療器材並未改變糖尿病需控制飲食的本質。選項 D 錯誤，因為該裝置的功能是「遞送」胰島素，而非讓患者不再需要胰島素。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'burden' refer to in the context of the patients' experience?

options.A: The physical weight of the medical device.
options.B: The financial cost of the treatment.
options.C: The emotional and practical difficulty of managing a health condition.
options.D: The negative side effects caused by the insulin pump.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。在此語境中，'burden' 指的是患者在日常生活中管理糖尿病時所承受的繁重負擔與壓力。文章第二段提到該裝置能移除患者白天與夜晚的心理壓力，因此這裡的 burden 指的是這種照護上的困難。選項 A 錯誤，因為這是抽象概念而非重量。選項 B 與文章強調的 NHS 免費提供或公平分配不符。選項 D 並非文章討論的重點。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: The NHS is struggling to distribute new medical technology to the public.
options.B: Artificial pancreas technology is a promising innovation that improves patient care and promotes equitable access.
options.C: Type 1 diabetes can now be completely cured through the use of smart sensors.
options.D: The medical technology industry is primarily focused on replacing doctors with computer algorithms.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。本文的主旨在於介紹人工胰臟技術如何透過自動化減輕患者負擔，並強調 NHS 在推廣此技術時，成功地實現了公平分配的目標。選項 A 與文意相反，文章強調 rollout 正在成功進行。選項 C 過度誇大，文章僅提到管理（managing）而非治癒（cured）。選項 D 偏離主題，文章討論的是輔助醫療而非取代醫生。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
